mapping.b_element: two coherently split Bose-Einstein condensates released from a double-well

mapping.a_element: interference pattern arising from the overlap of the two packets
mapping.b_element: spatial matter-wave interference fringes formed upon release

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic phenomenon of two overlapping wave packets producing interference fringes; the actual machinery differs entirely: paper A is a complex quantum Hamilton-Jacobi analysis of complex-plane trajectory topology governed by caustic and vortex singularities, whereas paper B is an experimental atom interferometer whose mechanism is coherent condensate splitting and relative-phase evolution read out from the fringe pattern, with no complex-plane trajectories, caustics, or vortices to map onto.
